content.problemStatement:
You can do the following operation on an array A any number of times:


Select two indices, 1≤l≤r≤∣A∣1\leq l \leq r \leq |A|1≤l≤r≤∣A∣ where ∣A∣|A|∣A∣ is the size
of array A.
For each l≤i≤rl\leq i \leq rl≤i≤r , assign Ai:=Ai⊕(i−l+1).†A_i:=A_i \oplus (i-l+1). ^{\dagger}Ai​:=Ai​⊕(i−l+1).†
Define a binary function FFF on an array AAA, where:

F(A)={1if it is possible to make all elements of A equal after any number of operations (including zero),0otherwise.F(A) = 
\begin{cases} 
1 & \text{if it is possible to make all elements of } A \text{ equal after any number of operations (including zero),} \\
0 & \text{otherwise.}
\end{cases}F(A)={10​if it is possible to make all elements of A equal after any number of operations (including zero),otherwise.​You are given an array BBB of length NNN , compute the sum of FFF over all subarrays of BBB.

†⊕^{\dagger} \oplus†⊕ represent bitwise **XOR** operation.


Input Format
The first line contains a single integer, TTT - the number of testcases. For each testcase,



the first line contains single integer, NNN - the size of array BBB.
the second line contains NNN space-separated integers, B1,B2,B3,⋯ ,BNB_1, B_2, B_3, \cdots, B_NB1​,B2​,B3​,⋯,BN​ - the elements of array BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line containing single integer −-− the sum of FFF over all subarrays of BBB.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all testcases doesn't exceed 2×1052\times10^52×105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Testcase 111:

F([5])=1F([5]) = 1F([5])=1, since all elements are already equal
F([5])=1F([5]) = 1F([5])=1, since all elements are already equal
F([100])=1F([100]) = 1F([100])=1, since all elements are already equal
F([5,5])=1F([5, 5]) = 1F([5,5])=1, since all elements are already equal
All other subarrays have FFF as 000.

Testcase 222:

F([6,7])=1F([6, 7]) = 1F([6,7])=1, since we can choose lll and rrr as 222 and 222 respectively, which changes [6,7][6, 7][6,7] to [6,6][6, 6][6,6].
